title: Contribution Margin Analysis: A company has total sales of $200,000, variable costs of...
summary: Calculate the contribution margin ratio as the proportion of sales remaining after variable costs.
prompt: A company has total sales of $200,000, variable costs of $120,000, and total fixed costs of $50,000. What is the contribution margin ratio?
solution: Contribution Margin Ratio = (Sales - Variable Costs) / Sales = ($200,000 - $120,000) / $200,000 = 0.40 or 40%. This ratio helps in understanding the proportion of sales that contributes to covering fixed costs and generating profit (Page 47).
